description:
## Background

`src-tauri/src/settings.rs` contains the versioned App Settings service and store adapter tests, while `src-tauri/src/dev_bridge.rs` contains the development bridge tests. Both files keep their test bodies inline even though their production concerns are distinct from their test fixtures and assertions.

## What to build

Move the inline App Settings and development-bridge test suites into separate outlined test modules. The implementation files should be readable without embedded test bodies, while durable settings and bridge behavior remain covered exactly as today.

## Technical Approach

Keep one `#[cfg(test)] mod tests;` declaration in each production module. Add local outlined roots under `src-tauri/src/settings/` and `src-tauri/src/dev_bridge/`. Keep settings serialization/store fixtures, bridge configuration inputs, and test-only support local to their corresponding module; do not create a generic infrastructure-test crate.

## Considerations

- Preserve App Settings versioning, defaulting, persistence-failure, and serialization behavior.
- Preserve development bridge coverage without changing production initialization or debug/release behavior.
- This is organization-only: no settings schema, RPC binding, plugin configuration, or end-to-end behavior changes.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `settings.rs` and `dev_bridge.rs` contain no Rust test bodies.
- [ ] Every current settings and development-bridge test remains present once in its outlined module.
- [ ] Production visibility, initialization behavior, and serialized settings format are unchanged.
- [ ] Focused infrastructure tests and full `cargo test` pass.

## Test Plan

- Run focused settings and development-bridge test filters while migrating.
- Run `cargo test` from `src-tauri`.
- Verify no `#[test]` body remains in either implementation file.

## Triage

ready-for-agent
title: Outline backend infrastructure tests
